32.title: Quality walkthrough — Apas
32.summary:
Executive Summary:

A critical alignment deviation was identified in the utility area of Tower T4, Floor 4, Flat 4. Structural elements exhibit significant misalignment of supporting props and members, compromising verticality. This condition poses a direct risk to the structural integrity and load distribution of the slab cast on 2026-04-28. Immediate corrective action and a structural stability assessment are required to mitigate potential failure.

36.title: Quality walkthrough — Apas
36.summary: The quality walkthrough for Project Apas identified critical structural and finishing non-conformances across Towers T3 and T4. Key findings include a critical alignment deviation in Tower T4 (Floor 4) affecting structural integrity and load distribution. Major surface irregularities and reinforcement exposure were documented in multiple units across Floors 7, 10, and 11. Several items remain pending third-party verification or have been rejected. Immediate corrective action is required to rectify the verticality deviation in T4 and address reinforcement exposure to prevent further degradation. Comprehensive remedial works and verified inspections are recommended for all identified areas to ensure compliance with engineering specifications.

37.title: Quality walkthrough — Apas
37.summary: The quality walkthrough for Project Apas identified several critical structural and finishing defects across Towers T1, T2, T6, T8, and T9. Key concerns include critical cracking and active water ingress on slabs cast on 2026-04-28, significant alignment deviations in Tower T9, and loss of sectional integrity due to honeycombing in Tower T2. Additionally, surface irregularities were noted in Tower T8. Immediate technical evaluations are required to determine the root causes of the structural distress and leakage. Corrective actions must be implemented to restore design tolerances and ensure watertight integrity before proceeding with subsequent finishes.

38.title: Apas Report
38.summary: The quality walkthrough for Project Apas identified several critical structural and finishing deficiencies across Towers T1, T2, T6, T8, and T9. Key findings include critical alignment deviations in T9 and structural cracking in T6, both requiring immediate technical evaluation. Active water ingress was documented in T1 and T6, while significant honeycombing and reinforcement exposure were observed in T2. Additionally, surface irregularities and leveling failures were noted in T8. Immediate corrective actions are required to address structural integrity concerns and waterproofing failures to ensure compliance with design specifications and safety standards.
